meta.name: Butter Scones (Biscuit-Adjacent Bakery Baseline)
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
meta.description: Classic butter scones: tender, lightly sweet, biscuit-adjacent crumb with crisp edges. Built as a bakery baseline with optional add-ins and a reliable cold-butter mixing method. Includes both commercial flour and fine fresh-milled flour guidance (choose one).

equipment.label: Box grater (optional, for frozen butter method)

ingredients.label: Commercial flour option (use this OR the fresh-milled option): all-purpose flour
Fresh-milled flour option (use this OR the commercial option): fine-milled, sifted
ingredients.notes: Sift coarse bran for tenderness. Fresh-milled absorbs more; you may need +15–45 ml extra cream/milk.

ingredients.label: Granulated sugar
ingredients.notes: About 1/4 cup + 1 tbsp. For less sweet scones, use 40 g.

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter, very cold
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 cup (1 stick). Cold butter creates flake/tenderness.

ingredients.label: Heavy cream (plus more for brushing)
ingredients.notes: About 1 cup. Substitute: 180 ml milk + 60 ml cream.

ingredients.label: Extra cream/milk (fresh-milled adjustment)
ingredients.notes: Add 15 ml at a time if dough is too dry; up to ~60 ml total depending on flour.

ingredients.label: Optional add-ins (choose one family)
ingredients.notes: Keep add-ins to ~120–180 g total to avoid heavy/dense scones.

ingredients.label: Coarse sugar (optional, for topping)
ingredients.notes: Sprinkle before baking for bakery-style crunch.

steps.title: Preheat and prep pan
steps.body: Heat oven to 400°F (205°C). Line a sheet pan with parchment. Keeping everything cold improves texture.

steps.title: Choose your flour option
steps.body: Use either the commercial flour option OR the fresh-milled flour option (not both). For fresh-milled flour, fine-mill and sift coarse bran for best tenderness.

steps.title: Mix dry ingredients
steps.body: In a large bowl, whisk chosen flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.

steps.title: Cut in cold butter
steps.body: Cut butter into small cubes (or grate frozen butter). Work butter into dry ingredients until you have pea-sized pieces plus some flatter shards. Those butter pieces create flake and tenderness.

steps.title: Mix wet ingredients
steps.body: In a small bowl, whisk cream, egg, and vanilla (if using).

steps.title: Combine (minimal mixing)
steps.body: Pour wet into dry and fold just until a shaggy dough forms. If adding mix-ins, fold them in now with minimal turns. Do not knead—overworking makes tough scones.

steps.title: Adjust dough for fresh-milled flour (if needed)
steps.body: If using fresh-milled flour and dough is dry/crumbly and won’t come together, add extra cream/milk 15 ml at a time until it holds together when pressed. If dough is sticky, dust lightly with flour.

steps.title: Pat, fold (lamination-lite), and shape
steps.body: Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface. Pat into a 2 cm (3/4 in) thick rectangle, fold into thirds, rotate, and pat again. Repeat once more for a more layered crumb (optional but recommended). Pat into a circle about 20 cm (8 in) wide and ~2.5 cm (1 in) thick.

steps.title: Cut and chill
steps.body: Cut into 8 wedges (or 10 smaller wedges). Transfer to the pan with space between. Chill 10–15 minutes (or freeze 5–10 minutes) to firm butter for better rise.

steps.title: Brush and top
steps.body: Brush tops lightly with cream. Sprinkle coarse sugar if using.

steps.title: Bake
steps.body: Bake 16–22 minutes until risen and golden brown on edges and tops. Avoid overbaking; scones should be tender.

steps.title: Cool
steps.body: Cool on pan 5 minutes, then move to a rack. Best served warm or same day.

notes: Biscuit-adjacent logic: cold butter + minimal mixing = tenderness; optional folds add light layering without full lamination.
Fresh-milled flour: sift coarse bran and expect higher absorption; a small liquid adjustment may be needed.
Make-ahead/freezer: Freeze unbaked scones on a tray, then bag airtight. Bake from frozen: add 2–5 minutes to bake time.
Flavor variants: lemon zest + blueberries, cinnamon chip, cranberry-orange, chocolate chunk—keep add-ins moderate.

storage.refrigerator: Airtight: up to 4 days (rewarm for best texture).
storage.freezer: Baked: up to 2 months. Unbaked: up to 2 months; bake from frozen.
